agents.name: Planner
agents.lastMessage: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position

report.hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3 and TM5/TM6, while reducing off-target binding to SSTR3/5 by avoiding general electrostatic complementarity.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current designs are not achieving sufficient structural quality to pass QC,

report.hypothesis: The current designs are not achieving sufficient structural quality to pass QC, despite good selectivity. Tightening the ddG gate and introducing targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions should improve both structural quality and selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: The current ddG threshold is too lenient. Tightening it to -20.0 will filter out weaker binders and improve the structural quality of the designs.

To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved pocket contacts in off-targets (SSTR3/SSTR5) at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing mutations on these positions will help push Δmargin further positive.
